claim: Users can create and inspect workflows through natural language, templates, and a visual graph.
deploymentGate: Confirm authenticated create, save, reload, edit, and delete against production Firebase.

claim: Every normalized workflow has exactly one Mission connected to its Trigger.
deploymentGate: Run one legacy workflow migration against a production copy.

claim: Stanley can combine deterministic browser steps, native API operations, generated connectors, and bounded Agent nodes.
deploymentGate: Run credentialed smoke tests for one browser flow, one native operation, one generated connector, and one Agent flow.

claim: Browser interactions escalate from exact selectors to semantic matching and optional vision, with reviewable selector learning.
deploymentGate: Exercise all three locator tiers in the deployed browser runtime with Gemini configured.

claim: Runs support admission checks, retries, checkpoints, cancellation, resumable state, evidence, and exception review.
deploymentGate: Force a mid-run failure, cancellation, and resume against deployed storage and queues.

claim: Credentials are scoped and resolved at execution time instead of being embedded in generated connector source or model prompts.
deploymentGate: Verify production IAM, encryption keys, secret access, redaction, and tenant isolation.

claim: Stanley ships reusable, structurally valid templates with Mission context.
deploymentGate: Create and run at least one browser and one API template in production.

claim: Connector, trust, monitoring, artifact, and workflow operations have web routes and user-facing workbenches.
deploymentGate: Confirm every Operations navigation route returns JSON through the deployed frontend-to-backend configuration.

claim: New users receive ten successful runs before a subscription is required.
deploymentGate: Use a fresh production account to verify failed runs do not consume quota and the eleventh successful run is gated.

claim: Stanley can convert conversational requests into clarification questions or validated, revision-aware workflow commands without directly persisting or executing model output.
deploymentGate: Deploy the planner endpoint, enable VITE_CONVERSATION_PLANNER_ENABLED, and run credentialed create, clarify, edit, explain, repair, and approval-diff canaries.

claim: Stanley can persist the exact workflow changes a user reviewed through an Admin-only proposal ledger, fingerprint-bound approval, command revalidation, transactional revision checks, idempotent replay, prior-version archival, and resulting-graph validation without executing the workflow.
deploymentGate: Deploy both conversation endpoints, approve a new workflow and an edit, then verify duplicate approval is idempotent and a stale revision is rejected against production Firestore.

claim: The production V2 Maps and Pinterest ads map to executable, count-matched templates that load dynamic feeds, capture links deterministically, enrich detail pages, and fail closed when the promised output contract is not met.
deploymentGate: Create both templates in production and run certification/advertised-canaries.example.json against live Google Maps and Pinterest sessions; publish exact-count ads only after both canaries pass.
